task_completion.description: The release pipeline is created and named 'Tailwind-CD'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Azure DevOps Release Pipeline editor or pipelines list, the pipeline name is clearly visible as 'Tailwind-CD'.

task_completion.description: The pipeline is linked to the 'Tailwind-CI' build artifact.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the pipeline editor's 'Artifacts' section, there is an artifact box showing 'Tailwind-CI' as the source build definition.

task_completion.description: The Continuous Deployment trigger is enabled for the artifact.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The continuous deployment trigger icon (lightning bolt) on the artifact box is shown as enabled (usually a filled icon or checkmark), or the trigger configuration panel shows the toggle set to 'Enabled'.

task_completion.description: Two sequential stages named 'Staging' and 'Production' are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the pipeline editor's 'Stages' section, there are two stage boxes named 'Staging' and 'Production', with 'Production' visually connected to follow 'Staging'.

task_completion.description: Both stages contain a PowerShell task running the inline script 'Write-Host 'Deploying...''
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the tasks view for the stages, a PowerShell task is present, its type is set to 'Inline', and the script text box contains exactly `Write-Host 'Deploying...'`.

task_completion.description: Pre-deployment approval is configured for the 'Production' stage with 'Docker' as the approver.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the pre-deployment conditions panel for the 'Production' stage, the 'Pre-deployment approvals' toggle is enabled, and the user 'Docker' is listed in the approvers field.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Azure DevOps web interface to create the pipeline.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Azure DevOps web portal being navigated, buttons being clicked, and forms being filled out.

integrity.description: The agent did not just type the answers into a text editor or mockup.
integrity.visual_evidence: The actions take place within the actual Azure DevOps application interface in a web browser.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a terminal or script to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal/command prompt is not used to run Azure CLI or REST API commands to create the pipeline.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Project Name: TailwindTraders. Pipeline Name: Tailwind-CD. Build Artifact Source: Tailwind-CI. Stage 1 Name: Staging. Stage 2 Name: Production. Required Approver: Docker.
